1. A 19-year-old college student presents with a 3-day history of sore throat, fever, and fatigue. On exam, he has exudative tonsillar enlargement, posterior cervical lymphadenopathy, and mild splenomegaly. A heterophile antibody test is positive. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
Supportive care with rest and analgesics
Infectious mononucleosis is caused by Epstein-Barr virus (EBV). Classic findings include exudative pharyngitis, posterior cervical lymphadenopathy, fatigue, and splenomegaly. A positive heterophile antibody (Monospot) test confirms the diagnosis. Treatment is supportive. Patients should avoid contact sports due to risk of splenic rupture.
